rulings.Case Title: GRADY FRAZIER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 26, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Honeywell International Inc.¿s motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute pursuant to C.C.P. 583.310, 583.360 is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless defendant notifies plaintiff of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Defendant must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss All Claims Of Plaintiff Myron Youngbluth Against It Pursuant To C.C.P. § 1030(D
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 26, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT JAMES HANSON VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 26, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 8. Defendant Collins Electrical Company Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment: the parties must appear at 9:45a.m. to be informed of the court¿s ruling. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GARY KASE et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 26, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Sepco Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Kase was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DARRELL CONNOLLY VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED

The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS LEO DOUGLAS TREMBLAY VS. ADERHOLT SPECIALTY COMPANY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File An Amendment To The Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 26, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Plaintiffs¿ motion for leave to file an amendment to the second amended complaint to add Terry Tremblay as a plaintiff is denied. Plaintiffs failed to show that Terry Tremblay is a proper plaintiff to this action and are further judicially estopped from adding him as a named plaintiff due to his involvement in a different lawsuit where he has been found to be a proper son and heir to his biological father, Ronald Martin. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
